commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=6adc26f881cf3e9dd941a484441f0ab4765a0fbc)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the Claude live `can_use_tool` allow response from `behavior: "allow"` to `updatedInput` and updates three spawn-test assertions to expect the input payload.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests 0. Total +1 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main emits the old allow payload and the linked issue gives a concrete OpenClaw plus Claude Code 2.1.156 ZodError path. I did not run a live Claude CLI session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the code defect is narrow but the external contributor proof gate and canonical sibling choice cannot be satisfied by an automated repair alone.

target.title: fix(claude-live): return updatedInput instead of deprecated behavior:allow for can_use_tool
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

OpenClaw 2026.6.8 is incompatible with `@anthropic-ai/claude-code@2.1.156`. Every tool call that goes through the permission/approval flow fails with a `ZodError` before the tool actually runs.

Claude Code 2.1.x tightened the `PermissionResult` schema. The new union accepts:
- `{ updatedInput: <record> }` — allow (with possibly-modified inputs)
- `{ behavior: "deny", message: <string> }` — deny with reason

OpenClaw's bridge was still returning the older shape `{ behavior: "allow" }` (no `updatedInput`), which validates against neither branch and causes ZodError.

Pinning Claude Code to 2.0.77 restores functionality, confirming the regression is on the OpenClaw bridge side.

## Evidence

### Fix (claude-live-session.ts, line 875)

The `handleClaudeLiveControlRequest` function now returns `{ updatedInput: input }` for allow responses instead of the deprecated `{ behavior: "allow" }`:

```diff
   response: allowed
     ? {
-        behavior: "allow",
+        updatedInput: input ?? {},
         ...(toolUseId ? { toolUseID: toolUseId } : {}),
       }
```

The deny path (`behavior: "deny"`) is unchanged — Claude Code 2.1.x still accepts that branch of the union.

### Test coverage (cli-runner.spawn.test.ts)

Three `can_use_tool` allow-path tests updated to verify the new `updatedInput` field:

| Test case | Assertion |
|-----------|----------|
| allow with exec policy full/no-ask | `updatedInput` equals `{ command: "ls" }` |
| allow with no exec policy (default) | `updatedInput` equals `{ command: "echo hi" }` |
| allow with YOLO bypassPermissions | `updatedInput` equals `{ command: "ls" }` |

All deny-path tests unchanged — deny still uses `behavior: "deny"` shape.

### Compatibility

- Claude Code 2.0.x: `updatedInput` was accepted as an alternative to `behavior: "allow"` in 2.0.x, so this is backwards-compatible
- Claude Code 2.1.x: `updatedInput` is the required shape; `behavior: "allow"` is rejected

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: pengxuewu-lab
